threads.ui:help: CPU threads for 10x bamtofastq. More threads = faster conversion but higher cost.

reads_per_fastq.ui:help: Maximum reads per output FASTQ chunk. The default (50,000,000) matches bamtofastq's own default; lower values produce more, smaller files.

flatten.ui:help: If enabled, all output FASTQs land in the output dataset root prefixed with the BAM basename (e.g. sampleA_S1_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz). If disabled, each BAM gets its own subdirectory.

memory_gb.ui:help: RAM allocated per BAM conversion task.

disk_gb.ui:help: Worker disk allocation per task. Should be roughly 4-5x the size of the largest BAM to hold the BAM + extracted FASTQs.

form.title: BAM to FASTQ (Cell Ranger)
form.description: Convert 10x Cell Ranger BAM files back to FASTQ using 10x Genomics bamtofastq. Discovers all BAMs recursively across one or more input datasets and emits a single combined output dataset. Auto-detects paired-end/single-end/index reads from the BAM header.

threads.title: CPU Threads
threads.description: Number of CPU threads per BAM conversion (4-16 recommended).

reads_per_fastq.title: Reads per FASTQ chunk
reads_per_fastq.description: Maximum reads per output FASTQ file. Lower values produce more, smaller files.

flatten.title: Flatten directory structure
flatten.description: Place all FASTQs in the output dataset root, prefixed with the BAM basename. Useful when feeding the output directly into valinor-cellranger.

memory_gb.title: Memory (GB)
memory_gb.description: RAM allocated per BAM conversion task.

disk_gb.title: Disk (GB)
disk_gb.description: Worker disk allocation per task. Scale up for very large BAMs.
